author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
title: The Ninth Breviate.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Chapter IV.—(9.)

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: The Ninth Breviate.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “The next question we shall require to be solved,” says he, “is this: By what means is it brought about that man is with sin?—by the necessity of nature, or by the freedom of choice?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: If it is by the necessity of nature, he is blameless; if by the freedom of choice, then the question arises, from whom he has received this freedom of choice.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: No doubt, from God.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Well, but that which God bestows is certainly good.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: This cannot be gainsaid.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: On what principle, then, is a thing proved to be good, if it is more prone to evil than to good?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For it is more prone to evil than to good if by means of it man can be with sin and cannot be without sin.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: The answer is this: It came by the freedom of choice that man was with sin; but a penal corruption closely followed thereon, and out of the liberty produced necessity.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Hence the cry of faith to God, “Lead Thou me out of my necessities.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: With these necessities upon us, we are either unable to understand what we want, or else (while having the wish) we are not strong enough to accomplish what we have come to understand.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Now it is just liberty itself that is promised to believers by the Liberator.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “If the Son,” says He, “shall make you free, ye shall be free indeed.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For, vanquished by the sin into which it fell by its volition, nature has lost liberty.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Hence another scripture says, “For of whom a man is overcome, of the same is he brought in bondage.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Since therefore “the whole need not the physician, but only they that be sick;” so likewise it is not the free that need the Deliverer, but only the enslaved.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: Hence the cry of joy to Him for deliverance, “Thou hast saved my soul from the straits of necessity.”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: For true liberty is also real health; and this would never have been lost, if the will had remained good.

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: But because the will has sinned, the hard necessity of having sin has pursued the sinner; until his infirmity be wholly healed, and such freedom be regained, that there must needs be, on the one hand, a permanent will to live happily, and, on the other hand, a voluntary and happy necessity of living virtuously, and never sinning.
